description_long: This object is a large, rectangular solar energy infrastructure installation, likely representing a rooftop or ground-mounted solar array. The surface is dominated by two prominent rectangular solar panel sections, each composed of a grid of photovoltaic cells, set into a flat, metallic base. Several raised black and gray structures, possibly representing inverters, junction boxes, or ventilation units, are distributed across the surface, adding functional complexity. The base platform is segmented with subtle color variations, including light blue and gray, and features a few marked zones and technical symbols. The overall design is industrial and utilitarian, with a focus on maximizing solar collection area while accommodating necessary electrical and structural components. The object appears robust, with a hard, smooth finish typical of metal and glass construction.
description: A large, flat platform with two rectangular solar panel arrays and several raised technical units, featuring a metallic and glass construction with industrial detailing.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad face of the platform with both solar panel arrays and multiple raised black and gray units distributed across the surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the depth of the platform and the alignment of the solar panels, with technical units visible along the edges.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back edge of the platform, with the solar panels and technical units seen from the opposite side, emphasizing the platform's width.
description_view_3: Opposite side view provides a clear look at the lengthwise arrangement of the solar panels and the distribution of raised units along the surface.
attribute_panel_orientation: flat, parallel to the base surface, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted or ground-mounted with a flat metallic frame structure
attribute_array_configuration: two rectangular arrays arranged side by side, each composed of a grid of photovoltaic cells
